items: Each extracted hook is under 200 LOC
No new files beyond the 6 extracted hooks (no unnecessary abstractions)
npx tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors after all changes
The UseTimelineDataResult interface is unchanged (identical public API)
All existing imports from useTimelineData resolve correctly (ClipTab, SaveStatus, RenderStatus, UseTimelineDataResult, shouldAcceptPolledData)
useTimelineState.types.ts compiles with ReturnType<typeof useTimelineData> unchanged
Video editor loads, edits, saves, and renders identically (manual verification)
git diff shows only moved code — no logic changes, no added/removed behavior

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 3 is incomplete: `useDerivedTimeline` cannot be a straight move of the `useMemo` blocks, because `resolvedConfig` currently depends on `configSignatureRef` and `stableResolvedConfigRef` to preserve object identity across poll churn. If that stabilization is dropped or left behind in the orchestrator, the split stops being a pure extraction and risks preview remount/regression.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `useTimelineData` keeps `configSignatureRef`/`stableResolvedConfigRef` at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L153) and uses them in the `resolvedConfig` memo at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L474). [RemotionPreview.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.tsx#L36) explicitly documents that it relies on `useTimelineData` keeping `config.clips` referentially stable.

unresolved_flags.concern: The plan does not preserve the dependency from save/sync to render state. `renderDirty` is not just render-local UI state; it is flipped to `true` when a save succeeds. Extracting `useRenderState` and `useTimelineSave` without explicitly threading that setter will change behavior.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `saveTimeline` sets `setRenderDirty(true)` on successful save at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L217), while `useClientRender` clears it after a render through the setter wired at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L616). Step 4/6 split these concerns but never specifies how the save hook will still drive `renderDirty`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Keeping `reloadFromServer` in `useAssetOperations` is the wrong boundary for this repository. That function is a save/sync reset path, not an asset helper, and moving it there forces the asset hook to depend on save internals anyway. This undermines the stated goal of concern isolation.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `reloadFromServer` at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L600) loads both timeline and registry, rebuilds full `TimelineData`, resets `editSeqRef`/`savedSeqRef`, calls `commitData`, preserves selection refs, and sets `saveStatus` to `'saved'` at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L613). The plan assumption says to keep it in `useAssetOperations`, which means the asset hook must reach deep into save/sync state instead of reducing coupling.

rationale: All three unresolved flags are correctness/boundary issues that would cause the executor to deviate from the plan during a "pure extraction" refactor. The plan's decomposition is sound, but the wiring details for cross-concern dependencies are underspecified. One iteration to incorporate the three fixes below into the plan text will prevent the executor from having to improvise on ref ownership, callback threading, and function placement — exactly the kind of decisions that should be explicit in a plan with a "no logic changes" success criterion.
signals_assessment: Iteration 1, weighted score 4.75, no prior trajectory to compare. Three significant flags all from critique_v1 — no recurring critiques or churn. Preflight clean. The flags are clustered around cross-concern wiring (refs, setters, function ownership) rather than structural disagreements with the 6-hook decomposition, so one targeted revision should resolve them without scope change.
warnings: The save hook is already the largest extraction (~180 LOC). Adding reloadFromServer to it may push it toward the 200 LOC limit — the revised plan should account for this.
Ensure the revised Step 3 explicitly lists configSignatureRef and stableResolvedConfigRef as items that move with useDerivedTimeline, not just the useMemo blocks.

settled_decisions.decision: Move `reloadFromServer` into `useTimelineSave`, not `useAssetOperations`. The asset hook should call `save.reloadFromServer()` via the orchestrator rather than reaching into save internals.
settled_decisions.rationale: Resolves flag `reload-misowned-in-asset-hook`. The function resets editSeqRef/savedSeqRef, calls commitData, and sets saveStatus — all save/sync concerns. Keeping it in the asset hook would force the asset hook to depend on save internals, defeating the decomposition goal.

settled_decisions.decision: The orchestrator passes `renderState.setRenderDirty` into `useTimelineSave` as a callback parameter so that `saveTimeline` can flip it on successful save.
settled_decisions.rationale: Resolves flag `missing-render-dirty-wiring`. This is the minimal threading — one setter callback — and keeps render state ownership in the render hook while allowing the save hook to signal dirty state.

settled_decisions.decision: `configSignatureRef` and `stableResolvedConfigRef` move into `useDerivedTimeline` alongside the `resolvedConfig` memo. They are part of the derived-data stabilization concern, not save/sync.
settled_decisions.rationale: Resolves flag `missing-resolved-config-stability`. RemotionPreview depends on referential stability of config.clips; the stabilization refs must travel with the memo that uses them.

orchestrator_guidance: First iteration; follow gate recommendation: ITERATE. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea: Split useTimelineData.ts (678 LOC) — the last god hook in the video editor. It mixes 6 concerns: (1) Data fetching/queries (timelineQuery, assetRegistryQuery, saveMutation) ~60 lines. (2) Save/sync lifecycle (saveTimeline, scheduleSave, commitData, applyTimelineEdit, applyResolvedConfigEdit, patchRegistry, editSeqRef, savedSeqRef, saveTimer, lastSavedSignature) ~200 lines. (3) Render state (renderStatus, renderProgress, renderLog, renderDirty, renderResult*, useClientRender wiring) ~60 lines. (4) UI preferences (scaleWidth, activeClipTab, assetPanel state, setters) ~50 lines. (5) Derived/memoized data (resolvedConfig, selectedClip, selectedTrack, compositionSize, renderMetadata, trackScaleMap) ~100 lines. (6) Asset operations (uploadAsset, registerAsset, uploadFiles, invalidateAssetRegistry, resolveAssetUrl, reloadFromServer) ~50 lines. Goal: each concern in its own hook under 200 LOC, useTimelineData becomes a thin orchestrator under 150 LOC that composes them. No behavioral changes — pure extraction.

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 3 is incomplete: `useDerivedTimeline` cannot be a straight move of the `useMemo` blocks, because `resolvedConfig` currently depends on `configSignatureRef` and `stableResolvedConfigRef` to preserve object identity across poll churn. If that stabilization is dropped or left behind in the orchestrator, the split stops being a pure extraction and risks preview remount/regression.

The plan does not preserve the dependency from save/sync to render state. `renderDirty` is not just render-local UI state; it is flipped to `true` when a save succeeds. Extracting `useRenderState` and `useTimelineSave` without explicitly threading that setter will change behavior.

Keeping `reloadFromServer` in `useAssetOperations` is the wrong boundary for this repository. That function is a save/sync reset path, not an asset helper, and moving it there forces the asset hook to depend on save internals anyway. This undermines the stated goal of concern isolation.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 4.75. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 3.
